free_text: Determining isotopic distributions of fission products
with a Penning trap
- final publ.
- prelim.
A 15 mg/cm2 U-238 target was used.
K-130 cyclotron
The on-line mass separator IGISOL.
The radioactive nuclides are produced in proton-induced
fission or heavy-ion fusion reactions by bombarding
a thin target with a primary beam from the Jyvaskyla
K-130 cyclotron. Radioactive ions are extracted from
the gas cell by helium gas flow and guided by the
sextupole ion guide into a differential pumping stage
where they are accelerated to 30 keV and mass-
separated with a 55 degree dipole magnet.
Correction for the radioactive decay losses
By CAJAD.
a microchannel plate detector
SB

digitized data superseded by new publ.(see Sub.003).
The normalization of isotopic yield distributions
is made by fitting a Gaussian shape to the isotopic
distribution and setting the areas under the
Gaussian equal.
Uncertainty due to statistical error and
decay correction only

From authors: The arbitrary unit of yield is chosen
such that it coincides with the cross-section in
millibarns as deduced from Huhta et al.(see REL-REF).
fig.14 from EPJ/A,44,147,2010
Total uncertainty
Uncertainty due to statistical error and
decay correction only
SD
The curves are normalized to each other at A = 119.
The yield of silver isotopes is calculated by
estimating the rate of the reference isotope by
linear interpolation between reference scans.
Uncertainty includes:
i) uncertainty of the intensity of the mass peak. For
most peaks this is just the statistical uncertainty.
There is an additional uncertainty for peaks whose
intensity is determined by fitting.
ii) Uncertainty due to applying the radioactive decay
correction. For very short half-lives with a large
uncertainty this actually becomes asymmetric, however
a symmetric approximation is used in this work.
iii) Uncertainty due to the ratio between irresolvable
isomeric states with different half-lives, when the
isotope is known to have isomeric states.
the uncertainty in the procedure of the
correction for the observed time structure of the
yield fluctuations
fig.13 from EPJ/A,44,147,2010
